sections.paragraph:
Βοῦρρος, ου, ὁ Burrus (both Gk. and Lat. forms of the name are well attested; cp. Hdb. on IEph 2:1), a ministrant (διάκονος) in Ephesus, termed σύνδουλος by Ign., IEph 2:1.—διὰ βούρου IPhld 11:2; likew. ISm 12:1 (v.l. Βόρρου).

Gk. Gk. = Greek

Lat. Lat. = Latin

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hdb. Hdb. = Handbuch (in connection with a specific NT book, the ref. is to a commentary in the series Handbuch zum Neuen Testament, founded by HLietzmann)—List 6

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

Ign. Ign. = Ignatius; always simply I. when cited with one of his epistles

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

likew. likew. = likewise

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 182
